title: View Tote Associated To A Rack In The RMS Map
summary: Use the RMS map container selection view to click a rack location and see which tote the system associates with that rack. The source specifically states that clicking a dark blue rack shows the tote on that rack, and that rack position labels should match what is shown in the map.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when you need to inspect a rack location in the RMS/map view and confirm which tote the system says is associated with that rack, including comparison to the physical tote label when visible.
not_for: Do not use this procedure as a recovery procedure for a tote mismatch; this source segment does not provide corrective actions.
Do not use this procedure to recover an empty rack condition; this source segment does not provide a recovery action when the rack is shown as empty.
Do not use this procedure to remove AGVs or perform other control actions not described in this source.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Use the RMS/container selection map view to inspect the rack and read the tote associated with the selected rack location.

safety_notes: This source supports viewing and inspecting rack/container information in the RMS map.
The broader training warns against taking unsupported control actions in the interface; this runbook is limited to inspection and comparison only.
access_or_tools_needed: Visibility of physical tote and rack labels if comparing system state to the floor

access_required: Access to the RMS web application/map view
system_preconditions: The rack location must be visible/selectable in the RMS/container selection map view
A dark blue rack state is required if you want the system to show the tote associated with that rack

visual_references.description: Training video frame showing rack location and container lookup concepts, including clicking a rack in container selection to inspect details.
Container Selection screen showing dark blue tote-present and light blue empty rack indicators.
Container Selection/tote lookup screen showing location selection and tote number display concepts.

steps.title: Open the RMS map and container selection view
steps.purpose: Access the map area used to inspect rack/container information.
steps.instruction: Open the RMS map and go to the container selection area so rack locations can be selected for inspection.
steps.expected_result: The container selection control and rack map are visible and ready for selection.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator can see the map and the container selection area used for rack inspection.
steps.failure_condition: The operator cannot access the RMS/map view or cannot locate the container selection area.

actions.label: Open container selection
actions.exact_action: Open the RMS map and go to the container selection area.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Screen area showing the container selection control and rack map.
Container Selection view and rack color indicators.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if you do not have access to the RMS/map view.
Escalate if the container selection area needed for rack inspection is not available.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: To look at more information about the rack, you have to click on the container selection and then physically click on the container itself.
Users access the RMS web application through a unique IP address with username and password.

steps.title: Select the rack location to inspect
steps.purpose: Choose the specific rack position whose associated tote you want to view.
steps.instruction: Select the rack location on the map that you want to inspect.
steps.expected_result: The intended rack location is selected in the map view.
steps.healthy_condition: The selected map object corresponds to the rack position you intend to check.
steps.failure_condition: The wrong map object is selected or the rack location cannot be selected.

actions.label: Select rack location
actions.exact_action: Select the rack location on the map that you want to inspect.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Rack locations in the map that can be clicked for more information.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if you cannot identify the intended rack position on the map.
Escalate if the map does not allow the rack location to be selected.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: To look at more information about the rack, you have to click on the container selection and then physically click on the container itself.
Rack locations are shown in the RMS/map view. To inspect a rack, the operator uses container selection and clicks the rack/container location to view more information about that rack.

steps.title: Click the dark blue rack to view associated information
steps.purpose: Reveal the tote associated with the selected rack when the source-supported rack state indicates a tote is present.
steps.instruction: If the rack is dark blue, click the rack to view its associated information.
steps.expected_result: The system displays information for the selected rack, including the tote on that rack.
steps.healthy_condition: The rack is dark blue and clicking it shows the tote associated with that rack.
steps.failure_condition: The rack is not dark blue, the rack appears empty, or clicking does not show tote information.

actions.label: Check rack color
actions.exact_action: If the rack is dark blue, click the rack to view its associated information.

actions.label: Open rack details
actions.exact_action: Click the rack to view its associated information.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Dark blue rack indicator meaning a tote is present.
Example of clicking a dark blue rack to inspect tote information.
Container selection screen showing dark blue tote-present and light blue empty rack states.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the rack is shown as empty; this source does not provide a recovery action.
Escalate if clicking a dark blue rack does not show the associated tote information.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: When a rack is dark blue, if you click on it, it will tell you what tote you have on the rack itself.
Dark blue indicates the presence of a tote and light blue represents an empty rack.
Selecting a dark blue rack in the map shows the tote associated with that rack.

steps.title: Read the tote identifier shown for the rack
steps.purpose: Capture the tote identity the system currently associates with the selected rack location.
steps.instruction: Read the tote identifier shown for that rack.
steps.expected_result: The tote identifier associated with the selected rack is visible to the operator.
steps.healthy_condition: A tote number or identifier is displayed for the selected dark blue rack.
steps.failure_condition: No tote identifier is shown for the selected rack or the displayed information is unclear.

actions.label: Read tote identifier
actions.exact_action: Read the tote identifier shown for that rack.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Displayed tote number/identifier after selecting a location.
Selected location showing a box icon and tote number.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the selected dark blue rack does not display a tote identifier.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: When a rack is dark blue, if you click on it, it will tell you what tote you have on the rack itself.
Selecting a location shows a box icon and the tote number.

steps.title: Compare the displayed tote identifier to the physical tote label if needed
steps.purpose: Check whether the physical tote at the rack matches the tote the system reports for that rack.
steps.instruction: If needed, compare the displayed tote identifier to the physical tote label seen at the rack location.
steps.expected_result: The operator determines whether the physical tote label matches the tote identifier shown in the map.
steps.healthy_condition: The physical tote label matches the tote identifier displayed in the map.
steps.failure_condition: The physical tote label does not match the tote identifier displayed in the map.

actions.label: Compare system tote to physical label
actions.exact_action: Compare the displayed tote identifier to the physical tote label seen at the rack location.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Rack/tote lookup concept used to compare map state to physical labels.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the physical tote label does not match the tote shown in the map.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Every tote has a label, the robot has a label number, and every rack position has a label that should match what's in the map.

steps.title: Confirm the rack position label and map location naming
steps.purpose: Ensure the inspection was performed on the intended rack position and not a nearby or misidentified location.
steps.instruction: Use the rack position label and map location naming to confirm you are checking the intended rack position.
steps.expected_result: The operator confirms the selected map location matches the intended physical rack position.
steps.healthy_condition: The rack position label and map location naming align with the intended rack being checked.
steps.failure_condition: The rack position label or map location naming does not align with the intended rack position.

actions.label: Verify rack position identity
actions.exact_action: Use the rack position label and map location naming to confirm you are checking the intended rack position.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Rack location naming and selected rack position in the map.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the rack position label does not match the map location you selected.
Escalate if you cannot confidently confirm the intended rack position.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Every tote has a label, the robot has a label number, and every rack position has a label that should match what's in the map.

success_criteria: The operator can select the intended rack in the RMS/container selection map view.
For a dark blue rack, the system displays the tote associated with that rack.
The operator can read and confirm the tote identifier shown for the selected rack.
healthy_conditions: The rack is selectable in the RMS/container selection map view.
A dark blue rack displays tote information when clicked.
The displayed tote identifier matches the physical tote label when comparison is performed.
The rack position label matches the map location being inspected.
failure_conditions: The RMS/container selection view is not accessible.
The intended rack location cannot be selected.
The rack is shown as empty when a tote lookup is expected.
Clicking a dark blue rack does not show tote information.
The physical tote label does not match the tote shown in the map.
The rack position label does not match the selected map location.
escalation_guidance: If the physical tote label does not match the tote shown in the map, record the mismatch and escalate or continue with a source-backed recovery procedure from another source.
If the rack is shown as empty, stop this procedure; this source does not provide a recovery action.
If the interface does not show tote information for a dark blue rack, escalate for support review.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Primary training frame for rack selection and tote lookup behavior.
Container Selection screen with dark blue and light blue rack state indicators.
Container Selection/tote lookup screen showing tote number display concepts.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: When a rack is dark blue, if you click on it, it will tell you what tote you have on the rack itself.
To look at more information about the rack, you have to click on the container selection and then physically click on the container itself.
Every tote has a label, the robot has a label number, and every rack position has a label that should match what's in the map.

missing_details: The source does not provide a time estimate for this procedure.
The source does not define a corrective action for a tote mismatch.
The source does not define a recovery action when the rack is shown as empty.
The source does not specify production stop or LOTO requirements for this inspection activity.
The source does not define role boundaries beyond operator-level use of the view.
